item.title: Does Ticketmaster have a stranglehold on concert ticketing — or is it just ‘bringing joy’?
item.description:
Source: The Verge
In a downtown Manhattan courtroom on Monday, lawyers for the US Justice Department and 40 state and district attorneys general warned a jury that the concert industry was being squeezed by a monopolist: Live Nation-Tickemaster. After amassing dominance over ticketing and artists' use of large amphitheaters, lead DOJ counsel David Dahlquist alleged, Live Nation constructed […]
Matched: policy(+3), law(+3), court(+2), war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Inside the secret meeting that led to the AI political resistance
item.description:
Source: The Verge
In early January, a group of 90 or so political, community and thought leaders gathered in a New Orleans Marriott for a secret conference on artificial intelligence - so secret, in fact, that no one knew who else had been invited until they walked into the room. Church leaders and conservative academics were sitting next […]
Matched: ai(+3), artificial intelligence(+3), who(+2)

item.title: What Trump’s war on Iran means for the US energy crunch
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Fuel prices surged after the Trump administration launched strikes against Iran on Saturday, immediately raising questions about whether the war would increase energy costs for Americans, put more pressure on power grids, and push companies to pump out more oil and gas in the US. If conflict drags on, that could potentially play into Donald […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: The best AirPods deals you can get right now
item.description:
Source: The Verge
If you know where to look, you can often score deals on Apple’s ever-expanding AirPods lineup. Both the AirPods Max and AirPods 4 (with and without ANC) now consistently receive discounts, as do the newer AirPods Pro 3. And while more recent shopping events like Black Friday and Cyber Monday delivered some of the steepest […]
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: X says it will suspend creators from revenue-sharing program for unlabeled AI posts of ‘armed conflict’
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Creators who break the rules will get a three-month suspension, and if they continue to violate the policy, they'll be permanently banned.
Matched: policy(+3), ai(+3), who(+2)

item.title: Eight Sleep raises $50M at $1.5B valuation
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Eight Sleep said it was free-cash-flow positive in 2025, and plans to use the new funding for new products, global expansions, and clinical validation.
Matched: ai(+3), funding(+2)

item.title: Anker’s last-gen sleep buds are nearly 40 percent off ahead of daylight saving time
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Bad news: most Americans are about to lose an hour of sleep next week. Good news: if you have trouble falling (or staying asleep), Newegg is currently selling Anker’s Soundcore Sleep A20 earbuds for $113.99 ($66 off) when you use coupon code MMSF88 at checkout, which drops them to just $6 shy of their lowest […]
Matched: budget(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: AI companies are spending millions to thwart this former tech exec’s congressional bid
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
A tech billionaire-backed super PAC is spending $125 million to undercut candidates pushing for AI regulation. New York's Alex Bores, a former tech executive himself, is one of them.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Hacked traffic cams and hijacked TVs: How cyber operations supported the war against Iran
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
After U.S. and Israeli forces started bombing Iran, reports say cyber operations have disrupted communications, supported surveillance activities, and have been used in psychological operations.
Matched: war(+3), ai(+3)

item.title: Why AI startups are selling the same equity at two different prices
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Some AI founders are using a novel valuation mechanism to manufacture unicorn status.
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: Alibaba’s Qwen tech lead steps down after major AI push
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Reactions rippled through Alibaba's Qwen team after tech lead Junyang Lin stepped down following a major model launch.
Matched: ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: Just three companies dominated the $189B in VC investments last month
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Crunchbase data shows a record $189 billion of global venture capital flowed to startups last month, with AI startups nabbing 90% of the capital.
Matched: ai(+3), startup(+2)

item.title: Highguard is shutting down this month
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Another high-profile live-service game is shutting down soon after launch: this time it's the free-to-play squad shooter Highguard. Developer Wildlight Entertainment says that, even though Highguard reached 2 million players, the game will be shut down permanently on March 12th. That's less than two months after the game debuted on January 26th. "Despite the passion […]
Matched: ai(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: Anduril aims at $60 billion valuation in new funding round
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The funding round would come less than a year after Anduril's Series G, which closed in June with $2.5 billion against a $30 billion valuation.
Matched: ai(+3), funding(+2)

item.title: Apple’s rumored MacBook Neo, a lower-cost, colorful laptop, could launch this week
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
This would mark the first time Apple competes with cheaper hardware like the Chromebook.
Matched: war(+3), launch(+2)

item.title: Activision silences trusted Call of Duty leaker
item.description:
Source: The Verge
One of the most trusted leakers for Call of Duty will no longer share confidential information about the gaming franchise after being hit with a cease and desist order. TheGhostOfHope (also known to the community as "Hope") announced on X that he is complying with legal demands from developer Activision to "stop leaking and disseminating […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: A suite of government hacking tools targeting iPhones is now being used by cybercriminals
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Security researchers say exploits used by governments to hack into iPhones have been found to be used by cybercriminals. They warned of an emerging market for "secondhand" exploits.
Matched: war(+3)

item.title: Another Oracle outage is messing up US TikTok
item.description:
Source: The Verge
The US version of TikTok is once again experiencing issues due to an Oracle outage, just a month after coming back online from a similar outage in February. The service disruption, impacting Oracle's Ashburn, Virginia data center, began early Tuesday afternoon, with Downdetector reports spiking around 1PM Eastern. TikTok USDS confirmed in a post on […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Shark’s latest robot vacuum hunts stains with UV light
item.description:
Source: The Verge
The Shark PowerDetect UV Reveal is SharkNinja's latest robot vacuum and mop. A flagship model with a multifunctional dock that empties the dustbin and refills and washes its mop, the Reveal's signature feature is a UV light designed to "find" stains on your floors. It costs $1,299.99 and is available now. Combined with an RGB […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Claude Code rolls out a voice mode capability
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
Anthropic is stepping up its game in the AI coding space with the rollout of Voice Mode in Claude Code.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Android users can now share tracker tag info with airlines to help locate lost luggage
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
More than 10 global airlines now accept Find Hub locations as part of their baggage recovery process.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Android’s Find Hub adds iPhone-like luggage tracking links
item.description:
Source: The Verge
Google is rolling out an Android update that includes the ability to share the location of your missing luggage with an airline. It's similar to the luggage-tracking feature Apple brought to the iPhone, allowing you to view the location of your Find Hub tag or accessory on a map, and provide your airline with a […]
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: The new MacBook Pro laptops are as much as $400 more expensive than their predecessors. Thank the RAM shortage.
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
With the demands for more computers and data centers to power AI, the market is experiencing a shortage of RAM, causing memory prices to surge.
Matched: ai(+3)

item.title: Activist investor Elliott takes a $1B stake in Pinterest, betting on AI-driven growth
item.description:
Source: TechCrunch
The news comes as Pinterest has faced serious headwinds. Shares tumbled over the past year, with disappointing earnings, layoffs affecting 15% of its workforce, a declining ad business, and increased rivalry from AI chatbots.
Matched: ai(+3)
